assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check for deprecated API usage with 'kubectl get events --all-namespaces | grep -i deprecated' and mentions reviewing application manifests for outdated API versions

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions checking admission webhooks in the 'If Issues Persist' section, noting they might be causing latency

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated section on 'Resource Limits & Requests' with commands to check allocated resources and mentions that Kubernetes 1.32 may have stricter resource enforcement

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple commands to check kube-system pods: 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system', checking API server pods, etcd pods, and control plane components

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: While the response includes monitoring commands and mentions API server metrics with 'kubectl top pods', it does not include the specific 'kubectl get --raw /metrics' command

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response checks CNI plugin pods but does not specifically mention NetworkPolicy or service mesh components needing updates for the new version

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check events with 'kubectl get events --all-namespaces --watch' and 'kubectl get events --all-namespaces | grep -i deprecated', which would capture scheduling and readiness issues

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response does not mention HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) and their potential behavior changes in the new version
